coreResponsibility: A middleware/provider layer that integrates Kaltura OVP (Online Video Platform) and OTT (Cloud TV/Over-The-Top) backend APIs with the PlayKit JS Player, fetching and transforming media configurations, session management, and analytics/bookmark tracking services.

featureOwnership: OVP (Online Video Platform) media provider - fetches media entries, playlists, and entry lists from Kaltura OVP backend
OTT (Cloud TV) media provider - fetches assets and asset lists from Kaltura Cloud TV backend
Session management (KS - Kaltura Session) - anonymous and authenticated session handling
Analytics service - tracking playback events for OVP backend
Stats service - collecting statistics for OVP backend
Bookmark service - tracking playback position for OTT backend (resume functionality)
DRM license URL handling and configuration
Media source parsing (HLS, DASH, Progressive, Image, Document)
Playback context resolution and block action handling
DVR status management for live streams
dataHandling: MediaEntry - core media entity with sources, metadata, duration, type, poster, DRM, captions
MediaSources - HLS, DASH, Progressive, Image, Document streaming sources
MediaConfig - session info, sources, and plugin configurations
PlaybackContext - streaming URLs, DRM data, actions, bumpers
Playlist/EntryList - multiple media entries for sequential playback
DRM configuration - license URLs, schemes (Widevine, FairPlay, PlayReady), certificates
Bookmark data - playback position, bitrate, file information for resume

upstream: Kaltura OVP Backend API (baseentry, metadata, playbackcontext, playlist services)
Kaltura Cloud TV/OTT Backend API (asset, bookmark services)
Kaltura Session API (widget sessions, user authentication)
downstream: playkit-js (core player) - consumes MediaConfig for playback
kaltura-player-js - uses providers to load media before playback
playkit-js-kava - may use analytics service data
Any PlayKit plugin requiring media metadata or session information
externalApis: Kaltura API v3 - user.get (for anonymous session detection)
